spec_match.rationale: The live preview is faked using hexToRgba in JS instead of applying the native CSS alpha() function directly to the element.

interactive.rationale: Highly interactive with live color picker and opacity slider updating the preview and generated CSS.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb aligns well with the convenience of alpha() for overlays.

edge_cases.rationale: Faking the support with RGBA avoids handling the actual lack of support edge case natively.

references.rationale: Includes link to ChromeStatus and CSS Color 5 spec.

design_system.rationale: Consistent with the design system styles and layout.

openQuestions.title: Use native alpha() function in live preview
openQuestions.detail: The current implementation fakes the overlay rendering using a JavaScript hexToRgba helper. It should apply the native alpha() CSS function to the overlay element's background, and use @supports or JS capability detection to show a fallback warning if the browser does not support it.
